revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:玄室]]
[[File:Koumoridzuka Kofun 12.jpg|thumb|260px|{{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}} ({{ill|Soja, Okayama|ja|岡山県総社市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soja, Okayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) interior of the Gen-shitsu]] [[File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 04.JPG|thumb|180px|Interior of the Gen-shitsu in Sawada Otsuka Kofun]] '''Gen-shitsu''' (玄室) refers to the burial chamber in a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The term ''Gen-shitsu'' originally meant a "dark room," but in ancient China, it came to denote underground burial chambers. Following this tradition, the term was applied to describe chambers in {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, a reference in the 16th year of Emperor Kinmei states: "安みせむとは玄室（くらきや）に。" (''Kurakiya ni yasumisemuto wa'').
The boundary between the ''Gen-shitsu'' and the {{ill|Sendou|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendou|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} is called the ''Senmon'' (羨門). In a double burial chamber of a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, the rear chamber is called the ''Koushitsu'' (後室) or the ''Okushitsu'' (奥室), while the chamber near the entrance is called the ''Zenshitsu'' (前室).
The floor plans of ''Gen-shitsu'' vary, including rectangular, square, rounded square corners, {{ill|Shamisen-douharigata|ja|三味線胴張形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamisen-douharigata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and circular shapes. The walls often use a ''Morioshi'' (持送り) construction method, where stones are slightly shifted inward as they are stacked. Some chambers feature stones carved into shape. Within the ''Gen-shitsu'', various structures are included, such as {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, coffin platforms, stone partitions for separation, and ''Ishiyakata'' (石屋形), which are facilities along the rear walls for placing remains.
The paintings in tumuli like {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|qid=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun|1=Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} and {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|qid=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun|1=Kitora Tomb}}, classified as {{ill|Decorated kofun|en|Decorated kofun|qid=Q11626716|lt=Decorated kofun|1=Decorated kofun}}, are often found on the walls of the ''Gen-shitsu''. Some chambers depict the {{ill|Four Symbols|en|Four Symbols|qid=Q1073260|lt=Four Symbols|1=Four Symbols}} on the walls.
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == '' Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, eds. ''New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary.'' New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 '' Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, eds. ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 '' Keiji Nagahara, supervising editor. ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8
== See Also == * {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Sendou|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendou|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}
